name: ChromaDB
description: ChromaDB is an open-source embedding database purpose-built for AI applications. It runs embedded in Python by default with a developer-friendly API and built-in embedding function integration. Widely adopted in the LangChain and LlamaIndex ecosystems, ChromaDB stores documents alongside their embeddings and metadata, supports vector similarity search with metadata filtering, and provides collection-based organization. A client-server mode is available for production deployments, but the default experience requires no external process.
format_on_disk: SQLite-backed (Single File) when persistent; ephemeral in-memory by default
core_strengths: Zero-configuration embedded mode with no external process required
Built-in embedding function with automatic text-to-vector conversion
Collection-based organization for separating document groups
Metadata-aware filtering combined with vector similarity search
First-class integration with LangChain, LlamaIndex, and AI frameworks
Ephemeral in-memory mode for rapid prototyping and testing
best_use_cases: Semantic search over document collections with metadata
Prototyping AI applications with minimal infrastructure
Storing and querying embeddings for recommendation systems
Local-first AI data storage for desktop and CLI tools
remarks: Runs embedded by default with no server required; client-server mode (ChromaDB server) is available for production deployments. The default embedding function downloads the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model (~80 MB) on first use. Ephemeral in-memory mode is non-persistent — use PersistentClient with a path for durability. Large-scale deployments may benefit from running the ChromaDB server separately.
